cards.front: What is the adjective agreement rule in Spanish?
cards.back: Adjectives must match the noun in gender (masculine/feminine) and number (singular/plural). Most -o adjectives change to -a for feminine.
cards.grammarNote: El pan fresco (masc.) → La fruta fresca (fem.) → Los panes frescos (masc. pl.) → Las frutas frescas (fem. pl.)

cards.front: The bread is fresh. / The vegetables are fresh.
cards.back: El pan está fresco. / Las verduras están frescas.
cards.grammarNote: Fresco follows the -o/-a pattern: fresco (m.sg.), fresca (f.sg.), frescos (m.pl.), frescas (f.pl.).

cards.front: The chicken is delicious. / The salad is delicious.
cards.back: El pollo está delicioso. / La ensalada está deliciosa.

cards.front: The soup is salty. / The fries are salty.
cards.back: La sopa está salada. / Las papas fritas están saladas.

cards.grammarNote: Frito/frita/fritos/fritas follows the -o/-a pattern. 'Las papas fritas' (fried potatoes) is feminine plural because 'papas' is feminine.
Frío/fría has a written accent on the í in all forms to break up the diphthong: frío, fría, fríos, frías.
Picante is invariable for gender (same for masculine and feminine), but adds -s for plural: picante → picantes.

cards.front: a large coffee / a large pizza
cards.back: un café grande / una pizza grande
cards.grammarNote: Grande is invariable for gender: grande for both masculine and feminine. Add -s for plural: grandes. Before a noun, grande shortens to gran: 'un gran restaurante'.

cards.front: Where do descriptive adjectives go in Spanish — before or after the noun?
cards.back: Usually after the noun: 'el pan fresco', 'la sopa caliente'. (English puts them before: 'the fresh bread'.)
cards.grammarNote: A few adjectives like bueno and malo can go before or after and may change form: buen pan, una mala idea.

cards.front: Chili is spicy. (always true) / The soup is cold. (right now)
cards.back: El chile es picante. / La sopa está fría.
cards.grammarNote: Use ser for inherent qualities (el chile es picante — always). Use estar for current conditions (la sopa está fría — right now).

cards.back: un pescado fresco / unos tomates frescos
